#### strict Mode — The Most Commonly Misunderstood Setting

`strict: true` (default): `plugin.json` is the authority for component definitions. `marketplace.json` can only supplement metadata (descriptions, tags).

`strict: false`: The `marketplace.json` entry is the entire plugin definition. **In this mode, if both `plugin.json` and the marketplace entry declare components, they conflict and the plugin fails to load.**

```json
// ✅ Correct use of strict: false — no plugin.json, marketplace.json is everything
{
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "inline-tool",
    "source": "https://example.com/tool.zip",
    "strict": false,
    "skills": ["skill-a"],
    "hooks": []
  }]
}

// ❌ Trap: strict: false but plugin.json also declares components
// → Both definitions conflict → plugin fails to load
```
